channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russia and Iran have a troubled history despite their current alliance
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin is hosting his Iranian counterpart Friday for the signing of a broad pact between Moscow and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arctic blast poses grave danger to newborn calves
item.description: An arctic blast expected to hit the U.S. this weekend could put newborn calves at great risk of freezing. Let's hear from two local ranchers, as well as Talbott farms to see how this cold front will affect crops too.
item.dc:creator: Timber Schuman, KREX 5

item.title: Ash and other dangers mean LA area residents who fled fires a week ago won't be going home soon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It has been more than a week since two massive fires forced tens of thousands of people to flee their homes in the Los Angeles area, and officials said Thursday residents won't be going home soon.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden official says President won't enforce TikTok ban before leaving office
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Donald Trump vows to help ‘troubled’ Hollywood with Mel Gibson, Jon Voight and Sylvester Stallone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump wants to make Hollywood “bigger, better and stronger” and has cast Mel Gibson, Jon Voight and Sylvester Stallone as stars of what he is calling his “Special Ambassadors to a great but very troubled place, Hollywood, California.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden's pointed warning about the U.S. becoming an “ oligarchy ” of tech billionaires will be illustrated at Donald Trump's inauguration, when the world's three richest men will sit on the dais as Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Driver in Texas migrant smuggling run that led to the deaths of 53 people pleads guilty
item.description: A Texas truck driver charged in the deaths of 53 migrants inside a sweltering tractor-trailer with no air conditioning has pleaded guilty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Armed L.A. residents defy evacuation orders to defend homes from wildfire looters
item.description: Amid concerns over looters targeting wildfire victims’ homes, some residents are defying evacuation orders and choosing to protect their properties instead.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American accused of assaulting a Pennsylvania student is extradited from France to the US
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — An American accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” was extradited Thursday from France to the United States. Ian Cleary, 31, of Saratoga, California, arrived in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, SYLVIE CORBET and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden admin announces last round of student debt relief
item.description: The Biden administration announced its final round of student debt relief on Thursday, topping off the most loan forgiveness ever given in a presidency.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nazi flag-bearing man gets 8 years in prison for truck crashing near White House
item.description: A Missouri man who crashed a rental truck into barriers protecting the White House has been sentenced to eight years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas school closing due to lack of state funding, says district
item.description: The district said it also voted to discontinue its Spanish immersion program, which was offered at all but one elementary school.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Torres, NewsNation

item.title: College students charged in TikTok-inspired 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: WORCESTER, Mass. (AP) — Five Massachusetts college students appeared in court Thursday, accused of plotting to lure a man to their campus through a dating app and then seizing him as part of a “Catch a Predator” trend on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA authorities searching for at least 36 missing people as wildfire death count rises to 27
item.description: The death toll from the Los Angeles wildfires rose to 27 on Thursday, while at least 36 people remain missing, city and county authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: David Lynch, director of 'Twin Peaks,' 'Blue Velvet' and more, dies at 78, family says
item.description: David Lynch, the filmmaker and director behind such movies as “Eraserhead,” “Blue Velvet” and the TV series “Twin Peaks,” has died, his family announced in a message posted to his official Facebook page.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Banning cellphones in schools gains popularity in red and blue states
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas' Republican Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders and California's Democratic Gov.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American radicalized by ISIS expressed 'excitement' about traveling overseas to support terrorist group: FBI
item.description: A suspected North Carolina ISIS supporter is facing federal charges after he allegedly made plans to travel to Morocco in December 2024 to join the foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: The incident happened the same day that a firefighting aircraft had to be grounded while battling the Palisades Fire.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, NewsNation

item.title: Louisiana governor relocating homeless people ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: Louisiana authorities are clearing homeless encampments around the Caesars Superdome in New Orleans ahead of next month's Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Fetterman's approach to Trump stirs speculation
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman’s (D-Pa.) open-minded approach to President-elect Trump is fueling questions about his motives and vexing some of his fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance's Ohio hometown struggles with how to recognize the incoming vice president
item.description: MIDDLETOWN, Ohio (AP) — It was a month after her son's election as vice president of the United States and JD Vance 's mother wondered why the city of his birth had yet to recognize him.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: Live Updates: Trump nominees grilled by senators; Turner ouster draws bipartisan ire
item.description: Thursday marked another busy day of hearings for President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees as his inauguration approaches.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Man sought after 'So I raped you' Facebook message being extradited to US
item.description: French Appeal Court prosecutors said an American man accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said, “So I raped you," was being extradited Thursday to the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2025 Grammys to proceed with focus on wildfire relief. Here's what you can expect
item.description: The 2025 Grammy Awards are right around the corner, which means it is time to get those viewing party plans in action. Allow us to help.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LA wildfires: Aerial firefighters leader says flames should be 'wake up' call
item.description: United Aerial Firefighters Association head Paul Petersen said the wildfires ravaging California could be among the most devastating in U.S. history, and hopes they "wake up" lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Giuliani settles legal fight with former Georgia election workers and agrees to stop defaming them
item.description: Rudy Giuliani has settled with two Georgia elections workers in a deal they say let him keep his homes and personal belongings in return for compensation and a promise to never defame them again.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Eager visitors flock to see spectacular lava fountaining from Kilauea eruption in Hawaii
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — People were flocking to Hawaii Volcanoes National Park on Wednesday to get a glimpse of fountaining lava. The eruption that began Dec. 23 in a crater at the summit of Kilauea volcano has paused periodically.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina man dies of Legionnaires' disease after using bacteria-laden hot tub, lawsuit claims
item.description: Two weeks after using a hot tub in his South Carolina home, 72-year-old Wayn Van Der Plaats was fighting for his life, his body racked by a deadly bacteria that causes Legionnaires' disease.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US must not become complacent to a growing terrorism threat, a Counterterrorism Center official says
item.description: McLEAN, Va. (AP) — Brett Holmgren got woken up early on New Year's Day by alerts that a driver had plowed into a crowd of revelers in New Orleans. The rampage, which killed 14 people, was the deadliest attack on U.S. soil in years and was inspired by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4
